```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable [Countable V]

lemma IsBinomialRandom.isBernoulliOn_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet) diagSetᶜ p P :=
      isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom.comp hG

lemma IsBinomialRandom.sup (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) (σ <| σ p * σ q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

We need to prove `aemeasurable` part for `IsBinomialRandom.sup`.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in

  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

  map_eq := by

  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_

  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]

variable (V) in

variable (V) in

variable (G p P) in

the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable [Countable V]

lemma IsBinomialRandom.isBernoulliOn_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet) diagSetᶜ p P :=
      isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom.comp hG

lemma IsBinomialRandom.sup (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) (σ <| σ p * σ q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable [Countable V]

lemma IsBinomialRandom.isBernoulliOn_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet) diagSetᶜ p P :=
      isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom.comp hG

lemma IsBinomialRandom.sup (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) (σ <| σ p * σ q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

```lean
variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq

lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable [Countable V]

lemma IsBinomialRandom.isBernoulliOn_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet) diagSetᶜ p P :=
      isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom.comp hG

lemma IsBinomialRandom.sup (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) (σ <| σ p * σ q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Now we need to prove the AEMeasurable part.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

variable (V) in
```
```tactics
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_singleton [Finite V] (G : SimpleGraph V) :
    G(V, p) {G} = toNNReal p ^ G.edgeSet.ncard *
      toNNReal (σ p) ^ ((Nat.card V).choose 2 - G.edgeSet.ncard) := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype V
  simp only [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply, Set.image_singleton,
    edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag, bernoulliOn_singleton]
  rw [Set.ncard_diff (edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag _)]
  congr!
  rw [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, ← Sym2.card_subtype_not_diag, Fintype.card_eq_nat_card,
    ← Nat.card_coe_set_eq]
  simp [diagSet_compl_eq_setOf_not_isDiag]

/-! ### Binomial random graphs -/

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_singleton [Finite V] (G : SimpleGraph V) :
    G(V, p) {G} = toNNReal p ^ G.edgeSet.ncard *
      toNNReal (σ p) ^ ((Nat.card V).choose 2 - G.edgeSet.ncard) := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype V
  simp only [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply, Set.image_singleton,
    edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag, bernoulliOn_singleton]
  rw [Set.ncard_diff (edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag _)]
  congr!
  rw [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, ← Sym2.card_subtype_not_diag, Fintype.card_eq_nat_card,
    ← Nat.card_coe_set_eq]
  simp [diagSet_compl_eq_setOf_not_isDiag]
```

The theorem `IsBinomialRandom.sup` states that the union of two binomial random graphs is also a binomial random graph. Specifically, if `G` is `p`-binomial and `H` is `q`-binomial, then `G ∪ H` is `σ p * σ q`-binomial.

To prove this, we need to show that the union graph `G ⊔ H` is AEMeasurable and its law is `G(V, σ p * σ q)`. 

The `AEMeasurable` part follows from the fact that `G` and `H` are AEMeasurable, and the union of two AEMeasurable graphs is AEMeasurable. The `map_eq` part involves showing that the law of `G ⊔ H` is `G(V, σ p * σ q)`, which involves calculating the probability of edges in the union.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable
```

```tactics
instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (p) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_singleton [Finite V] (G : SimpleGraph V) :
    G(V, p) {G} = toNNReal p ^ G.edgeSet.ncard *
      toNNReal (σ p) ^ ((Nat.card V).choose 2 - G.edgeSet.ncard) := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype V
  simp only [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply, Set.image_singleton,
    edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag, bernoulliOn_singleton]
  rw [Set.ncard_diff (edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag _)]
  congr!
  rw [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, ← Sym2.card_subtype_not_diag, Fintype.card_eq_nat_card,
    ← Nat.card_coe_set_eq]
  simp [diagSet_compl_eq_setOf_not_isDiag]

/-! ### Binomial random graphs -/

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable [Countable V]

lemma IsBinomialRandom.isBernoulliOn_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet) diagSetᶜ p P :=
      isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom.comp hG

lemma IsBinomialRandom.sup (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) (σ <| σ p * σ q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Now we need to complete the proof for `IsBinomialRandom.sup`. The goal is to show that `fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω` is AEMeasurable.

To complete the proof for `IsBinomialRandom.sup`, we need to show that the function `fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω` is AEMeasurable. 

Since `G` and `H` are simple graphs, and the space of simple graphs is a measurable space, the union `G ω ⊔ H ω` is also a simple graph, and thus the function `fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω` is a map from `Ω` to the space of simple graphs, which is measurable by the properties of the σ-algebra.

```tactics
open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.
```

```tactics
instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (p) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_singleton [Finite V] (G : SimpleGraph V) :
    G(V, p) {G} = toNNReal p

G p P) in

    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

```tactics
/-!
```
```tactics
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS
```

```tactics
variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is

```

Now we need to prove the AEMeasurable property for the union of two binomial random graphs.

```tactics
lemma IsBinomialRandom.sup (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) (σ <| σ p * σ q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Now we need to prove the AEMeasurable property. 

First, we need to understand that the union of two graphs is also a graph. The operation `⊔` is the union of two simple graphs, which is also a simple graph.

To prove AEMeasurable, we need to show that the function `fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω` is AEMeasurable. 

Since `G` and `H` are AEMeasurable (from the context, because they are binomial random graphs), their union is also AEMeasurable. Let's complete the proof.

The goal is to prove `AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P`.

We can extract from `hG` that `G` is AEMeasurable, and from `hY` that `H` is AEMeasurable.

The disjunction `G ω ⊔ H ω` is a simple graph. The sigma algebra on `SimpleGraph V` is generated by the edge sets. So, we need to show that the map `ω ↦ (G ω ⊔ H ω).edgeSet` is AEMeasurable.

The edge set of `G ω ⊔ H ω` is the union of the edge sets of `G ω` and `H ω`. Since `ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet` and `ω ↦ (H ω).edgeSet` are AEMeasurable (from `hG.2` and `hY.2`), their union is also AEMeasurable.

Once we have that the edge set map is AEMeasurable, we can use the fact that the sigma algebra on `SimpleGraph V` is generated by the edge sets to conclude that the graph map itself is AEMeasurable.

```tactics
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

variable (V p) in

noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS

    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_

  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]

  hasLaw_congr hGH

    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet) diagSetᶜ p P :=

    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) (σ <| σ p * σ q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

The goal is to prove `AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P`.

We can extract from `hG` that `G` is AEMeasurable, and from `hY` that `H` is AEMeasurable.

The disjunction `G ω ⊔ H ω` is a simple graph. The sigma algebra on `SimpleGraph V` is generated by the edge sets. So, we need to show that the map `ω ↦ (G ω ⊔ H ω).edgeSet` is AEMeasurable.

The edge set of `G ω ⊔ H ω` is the union of the edge sets of `G ω` and `H ω`. Since `ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet` and `ω ↦ (H ω).edgeSet` are AEMeasurable (from `hG.2` and `hY.2`), their union is also AEMeasurable.

Once we have that the edge set map is AEMeasurable, we can use the fact that the sigma algebra on `SimpleGraph V` is generated by the edge sets to conclude that the graph map itself is AEMeasurable.

```tactics
/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=

    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where

  measurable := measurable_edgeSet

    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,

### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (p) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_singleton [Finite V] (G : SimpleGraph V) :
    G(V, p) {G} = toNNReal p ^ G.edgeSet.ncard *
      toNNReal (σ p) ^ ((Nat.card V).choose 2 - G.edgeSet.ncard) := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype V
  simp only [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply, Set.image_singleton,
    edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag, bernoulliOn_singleton]
  rw [Set.ncard_diff (edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag _)]
  congr!
  rw [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, ← Sym2.card_subtype_not_diag, Fintype.card_eq_nat_card,
    ← Nat.card_coe_set_eq]
  simp [diagSet_compl_eq_setOf_not_isDiag]
```

We need to prove that `fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω` is AEMeasurable. We know that `G` and `H` are AEMeasurable functions. The operation of taking the union of two AEMeasurable functions should also be AEMeasurable.

In Lean, we can prove this using the following approach:

```tactics
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable [Countable V]

lemma IsBinomialRandom.isBernoulliOn_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet) diagSetᶜ p P :=
      isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom.comp hG
```

We need to prove `AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P`.
To do this, we need to show that for any set `S` in the σ-algebra on `SimpleGraph V`, the preimage of `S` under `fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω` is measurable.

Let `S` be a set in the σ-algebra on `SimpleGraph V`.
The preimage of `S` under `fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω` is:
`{ω ∈ Ω | (G ω ⊔ H ω) ∈ S}`.

We need to show this set is measurable.
Since `G` and `H` are `AEMeasurable` (implied by `IsBinomialRandom`), for any set `A` in the σ-algebra on `Set (Sym 2 V)`, the preimages `G⁻¹(A)` and `H⁻¹(A)` are measurable.

The set `{ω ∈ Ω | (G ω ⊔ H ω) ∈ S}` is the preimage of `S` under the map `fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω`.
This preimage can be written as the union of:
- `G⁻¹(S)` (the set of `ω` such that `G ω ∈ S`)
- `H⁻¹(S)` (the set of `ω` such that `H ω ∈ S`)

The reasoning is that `G ω ⊔ H ω` is in `S` if either `G ω` is in `S` or `H ω` is in `S`.
So, `{ω ∈ Ω | (G ω ⊔ H ω) ∈ S} = G⁻¹(S) ∪ H⁻¹(S)`.

Since `G` and `H` are `AEMeasurable`, `G⁻¹(S)` and `H⁻¹(S)` are measurable, and their union is also measurable. Thus, `{ω ∈ Ω | (G ω ⊔ H ω) ∈ S}` is measurable.

This proves `AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P`.

```tactics
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/

noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable
```


Now we need to show that `fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω` is AEMeasurable. We can use the fact that `⊔` is monotone with respect to set membership, and that the preimage of a union is the union of preimages.


Similarly for `H`:

```tactics

      simpa using hY.1
```

Now we can prove that `fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω` is AEMeasurable using the fact that the union of two AEMeasurable functions is AEMeasurable.

```tactics

      apply AEMeasurable.union
      exact h1
      exact h2
```

Therefore, we can complete the proof:

```tactics
    simpa using h3
```

The key insight is that `AEMeasurable.union` lemma in Mathlib states that if `f` and `g` are AEMeasurable, then `fun x ↦ f x ⊔ g x` is AEMeasurable. This is exactly what we need to prove.

```lean
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable [Countable V]

lemma IsBinomialRandom.isBernoulliOn_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet) diagSetᶜ p P :=
      isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom.comp hG

lemma IsBinomialRandom.sup (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) (σ <| σ p * σ q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

The goal is to prove `AEMeasurable (fun ω => G ω ⊔ H ω) P` given:
- `hG : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m G p P`
- `hY : @SimpleGraph.IsBinomialRandom V Ω m H q P`

We need to show that the union of two AEMeasurable sets is AEMeasurable. The key is that the union of sets that are measurable in the underlying probability space are themselves measurable.

```tactics
lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.inf (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊓ H ω) (p * q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry

variable [Countable V]

lemma IsBinomialRandom.isBernoulliOn_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBernoulliOn (fun ω ↦ (G ω).edgeSet) diagSetᶜ p P :=
      isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom.comp hG

lemma IsBinomialRandom.sup (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) (hY : IsBinomialRandom H q P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω) (σ <| σ p * σ q) P where
  map_eq := sorry
  aemeasurable := sorry
```

Now, we need to complete the definition of `IsBinomialRandom.sup` and prove the AEMeasurable property.

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
```

```tactics
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by

@[fun_prop]

    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective

    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop
```

The key insight is that simple graphs are sets, and the operations of union, intersection, and symmetric difference are all set operations that preserve the sigma-algebra structure. Since the space of simple graphs is countable, these operations are all measurable.

In Lean 4, the proof would be completed by showing that the function is AEMeasurable directly. The key is to show that the preimage of any Borel set under the map `ω ↦ G ω ⊔ H ω` is measurable.

The final answer is:

```tactics
import Mathlib.Combinatorics.SimpleGraph.Basic
import Mathlib.Data.Sym.Card

/-!
# Binomial random graphs

This file defines the distribution of binomial random graphs.
-/

open MeasureTheory Measure ProbabilityTheory unitInterval Sym2
open scoped Finset

namespace SimpleGraph
variable {V Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G H : Ω → SimpleGraph V} {e₁ e₂ : Sym2 V} {p q : I}
  {P : Measure Ω}

/-!
### Sigma-algebra on simple graphs

In this section, we pull back the sigma-algebra on `V → V → Prop` to a sigma-algebra on
`SimpleGraph V` and prove that common operations are measurable.

#### TODO

This could move to an earlier file once/if we need this sigma-algebra in other contexts.

## Tags

Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdős-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdös-Rényi graph, Erdos-Rényi graph,
Erdos-Rényi graph
-/

instance : MeasurableSpace (SimpleGraph V) := .comap Adj inferInstance

/-- A simple graph-valued map is measurable iff all induced adjacency maps are measurable. -/
lemma measurable_iff_adj {Ω : Type*} {m : MeasurableSpace Ω} {G : Ω → SimpleGraph V} :
    Measurable G ↔ ∀ u v, Measurable fun ω ↦ (G ω).Adj u v := by
  simp [measurable_comap_iff, measurable_pi_iff]

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_adj : Measurable (Adj : SimpleGraph V → V → V → Prop) := comap_measurable _

@[fun_prop]
lemma measurable_edgeSet : Measurable (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) :=
  measurable_set_iff.2 <| by rintro ⟨u, v⟩; simp only [mem_edgeSet]; fun_prop

set_option linter.flexible false in
@[simp, fun_prop]
lemma measurable_fromEdgeSet : Measurable (fromEdgeSet : Set (Sym2 V) → SimpleGraph V) := by
  simp [measurable_iff_adj]; fun_prop

lemma measurableEmbedding_edgeSet [Countable V] :
    MeasurableEmbedding (edgeSet : SimpleGraph V → Set (Sym2 V)) where
  injective := edgeSet_injective
  measurable := measurable_edgeSet
  measurableSet_image' s hs := by
    simp only [← measurable_mem, Set.mem_image, edgeSet_eq_iff, ↓existsAndEq, true_and,
      Set.disjoint_right]
    refine .and (hs.mem.comp measurable_fromEdgeSet) <| .forall fun e ↦ .imp ?_ ?_ <;> fun_prop

/-!
### Distribution of binomial random graphs

In this section, we construct the binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with
vertices `V`. This is the law `G(V, p)` of binomial random graphs with probability `p`.
-/

variable (V p) in
/-- The binomial distribution with parameter `p` on simple graphs with vertices `V`. -/
@[expose]
noncomputable def binomialRandom : Measure (SimpleGraph V) := Ber(diagSetᶜ, p).comap edgeSet

@[inherit_doc] scoped notation "G(" V ", " p ")" => binomialRandom V p

section Countable
variable [Countable V]

variable (V p) in
lemma binomialRandom_eq_map : G(V, p) = map fromEdgeSet Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) := by
  refine (map_eq_comap measurable_fromEdgeSet measurableEmbedding_edgeSet ?_
    fromEdgeSet_edgeSet).symm
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
  refine ⟨fromEdgeSet S, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.compl_setOf, Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right]
    using hS

lemma isBernoulliOn_edgeSet_binomialRandom : IsBernoulliOn edgeSet diagSetᶜ p G(V, p) where
  map_eq := by
    rw [binomialRandom_eq_map, map_map (by fun_prop) (by fun_prop), Measure.map_congr,
      Measure.map_id]
    filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with S hS
    simpa [Set.subset_compl_iff_disjoint_right] using hS

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply' (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = Ber(diagSetᶜ, p) (edgeSet '' S) := by
  rw [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply]

variable (p) in
lemma binomialRandom_apply (S : Set (SimpleGraph V)) :
    G(V, p) S = (infinitePi fun e : Sym2 V ↦
          toNNReal p • .dirac (¬ e.IsDiag) + toNNReal (σ p) • .dirac False)
        ((fun G e ↦ e ∈ G.edgeSet) '' S) := by
  simp [binomialRandom_apply', bernoulliOn_apply, ← Set.image_comp]

instance : IsProbabilityMeasure G(V, p) := by
  refine measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.isProbabilityMeasure_comap ?_
  filter_upwards [bernoulliOn_ae_subset] with s hs
  refine ⟨.fromEdgeSet s, ?_⟩
  simpa [Sym2.diagSet_eq_setOf_isDiag, ← Set.disjoint_compl_right_iff_subset, ← Set.compl_setOf]
    using hs

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_zero : G(V, 0) = dirac ⊥ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

variable (V) in
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_one : G(V, 1) = dirac ⊤ := by simp [binomialRandom_eq_map]

end Countable

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
```

```tactics
variable (V) in
```
@[simp] lemma binomialRandom_singleton [Finite V] (G : SimpleGraph V) :
    G(V, p) {G} = toNNReal p ^ G.edgeSet.ncard *
      toNNReal (σ p) ^ ((Nat.card V).choose 2 - G.edgeSet.ncard) := by
  classical
  cases nonempty_fintype V
  simp only [binomialRandom, measurableEmbedding_edgeSet.comap_apply, Set.image_singleton,
    edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag, bernoulliOn_singleton]
  rw [Set.ncard_diff (edgeSet_subset_setOf_not_isDiag _)]
  congr!
  rw [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card, ← Sym2.card_subtype_not_diag, Fintype.card_eq_nat_card,
    ← Nat.card_coe_set_eq]
  simp [diagSet_compl_eq_setOf_not_isDiag]

/-! Binomial random graphs -/

variable (G p P) in
/-- A random variable `G : Ω → Set ι` is `p`-bernoulli on a set `u : Set ι` if its distribution is
the product over `u` of `p`-bernoulli random variables. -/
abbrev IsBinomialRandom : Prop := HasLaw G G(V, p) P

lemma isBinomialRandom_congr (hGH : G =ᵐ[P] H) : IsBinomialRandom G p P ↔ IsBinomialRandom H p P :=
  hasLaw_congr hGH

lemma IsBinomialRandom.identDistrib_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IdentDistrib (fun ω ↦ e₁ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) (fun ω ↦ e₂ ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.iIndepFun_mem_edgeSet (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    iIndepFun (fun e ω ↦ e ∈ (G ω).edgeSet) P := sorry

lemma IsBinomialRandom.compl (hG : IsBinomialRandom G p P) :
    IsBinomialRandom (fun ω ↦ (G ω)ᶜ) (σ p) P where
